methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Cellares is a Series C–stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector that has raised $350M+. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Ultima Genomics, Insitro, and Colossal Biosciences at the same stage.

Two methods produce actionable results. Stage-Progression applies a 1.7x multiplier to the $12M total-raised proxy, implying $20M. Peer-Median anchors to the valuation distribution of same-sector, same-stage private companies: Ultima Genomics at $4.0B, Insitro at $2.4B, and Colossal Biosciences at $1.5B, yielding a median of $2.4B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed. The public sector median P/S for Biotech & Health stands at 5.8x across the three tickers, but remains unusable without revenue visibility.

Triangulation across the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.2B (median) with bounds between $17M (low, stage-progression floor) and $2.8B (high, peer-range ceiling). The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around revenue scale and market positioning relative to the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include the target's current revenue run rate, customer concentration, and IP defensibility relative to better-capitalized peers like Ultima Genomics.
